title: macOS Filesystem Capacity from Splunk TA df
dataSources: `index=os` `sourcetype=df` from Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (per-host filesystem inventory)
app: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (`Splunk_TA_nix`, Splunkbase #833) with Universal Forwarder on macOS
spl:
index=os sourcetype=df host=*
| where MountedOn="/" OR match(MountedOn, "^/System/Volumes/Data$")
| eval use_pct=coalesce(tonumber(UsePct), tonumber(PercentUsed), round((UsedKB/SizeKB)*100,2))
| where use_pct > 90
| table _time, host, Filesystem, MountedOn, SizeKB, AvailKB, use_pct
| sort - use_pct
description: macOS endpoints exhaust `/System/Volumes/Data` quickly with local Time Machine snapshots, Xcode caches, and mail attachments. The TA `df` input already exposes per-mount utilization; scoping to root and Data volumes catches impending fullness before logging and MDM operations fail.
value: Prevents OS instability and failed security tooling updates by alerting on critical mount points before they hit 100% utilization.
knownFalsePositives: APFS local snapshots and Time Machine locals can make `/System/Volumes/Data` look fuller than a Finder ‚ÄúGet Info‚Äù until snapshots are thinned. Network home directories or FUSE mounts can confuse `df` field parsing‚Äîtest on a pilot Mac. iCloud purge and Mobile Accounts can free space minutes after a noisy alert.
implementation: Enable the `df` scripted input in `Splunk_TA_nix` on macOS UF installs (Darwin is supported for many collectors). Confirm field extractions: prefer `UsePct`; if your build emits `UsedKB`/`SizeKB`, derive percent as in the SPL. Poll every 5‚Äì15 minutes. Exclude network mounts with a `where NOT match(Filesystem, "^//")` clause if noisy. Alert above 90% on `/` and `/System/Volumes/Data` (adjust for Silicon vs Intel layout).
visualization: Table (host, mount, use_pct), Line chart (use_pct trend for Data volume), Single value (count of hosts over threshold).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` avg(Performance.storage_used_percent) as used_pct
  from datamodel=Performance where nodename=Performance.Storage
  by Performance.host Performance.filesystem span=15m
| where used_pct > 90

references.title: Splunk Documentation: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux
Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (Splunkbase 833)

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ `Splunk_TA_nix` deployed to the UF with `df` enabled in `inputs.conf`.
‚Ä¢ Verify whether your TA version uses `UsePct` vs raw KB fields via Search `| head 1` on a pilot host.

Step 1 ‚Äî Add lookup for allowed minimum free space per department if needed.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and optional CIM variant

Primary SPL (as in this UC‚Äôs `spl` field) stays on `sourcetype=df` for macOS (and Linux) full-fidelity field math.

If the same data is CIM-tagged to **Performance.Storage** with acceleration:

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` avg(Performance.storage_used_percent) as used_pct
  from datamodel=Performance where nodename=Performance.Storage
  by Performance.host Performance.filesystem span=15m
| where used_pct > 90
```

Step 3 ‚Äî Schedule alert; throttle per host to once per hour.

Step 4 ‚Äî Validate by filling a test volume in a lab; compare to Finder and `diskutil apfs listSnapshots` when APFS snapshots distort free space.

Step 5 ‚Äî Pair with UC-1.3.x macOS crash monitoring when disks are critically full.
grandmaExplanation: We warn when a Mac‚Äôs main disk is almost full so it doesn‚Äôt freeze up or stop updating when space runs out.
